acceptance_criteria: SSE format matches mastodon.social wire format; WebSocket payload is double-encoded JSON string; delete event is string ID; all event types tested against oracle
description:
Define the StreamEvent enum and wire serialization. Event types: update (new status), notification, delete (status ID string), status.update (edited status), conversation (DM). Wire format for SSE: 'event: update\ndata: {json}\n\n'. Wire format for WebSocket: JSON object {stream: ['user'], event: 'update', payload: '{json}'} (payload is a JSON-encoded string, not nested JSON).

AGENT WORKFLOW: Spawn a research subagent to connect to mastodon.social's streaming endpoint (SSE) and capture real event payloads for each event type. Use these as oracle.

TEST DISCIPLINE:
- Serialize update event → SSE format matches mastodon.social oracle exactly
- Serialize delete event → payload is just the status ID string
- Serialize for WebSocket → payload field is a JSON-encoded string (double-encoded)
- Each event type tested against mastodon.social oracle
- delete event payload must be a bare string ID, NOT a Status object

DEFENSIVE PROGRAMMING:
- WebSocket payload double-encoding is intentional and required for compatibility — document clearly with a comment and a test
- Event serialization must never include private key material or auth tokens
title: StreamEvent: all event types with correct Mastodon wire format
